clusters.title: Watch: Couple scale Empire State Building, get engaged and are arrested
clusters.neutral_headline: Watch: Couple scale Empire State Building, get engaged and are arrested
bullet_summary: News footage showed the masked pair kissing atop the skyscraper before one knelt and the other appeared to display a ring
Reported by BBC World, CBS News, Guardian World and others

items.title: Watch: Couple scale Empire State Building, get engaged and are arrested
items.standfirst: The two were atop the 1,454 ft (443m) building for at least ten minutes and displayed a large banner before climbing down.
items.content: The two were atop the 1,454 ft (443m) building for at least ten minutes and displayed a large banner before climbing down.

items.title: Who climbed the Empire State Building? Here's what we know.
items.standfirst: The two people taken into custody for climbing New York City's Empire State Building where they apparently got engaged have been identified as Ivan Kuznetsov, 32, and Angela Nikolau, 33.
items.content: The two people taken into custody for climbing New York City's Empire State Building where they apparently got engaged have been identified as Ivan Kuznetsov, 32, and Angela Nikolau, 33.

items.title: 2 Empire State Building climbers in custody after apparently getting engaged at the top
items.standfirst: Two people climbed to the top of New York City's Empire State Building, unfurled a banner, and then apparently got engaged Wednesday afternoon.
items.content: Two people climbed to the top of New York City's Empire State Building, unfurled a banner, and then apparently got engaged Wednesday afternoon.

items.title: Two people arrested in apparent marriage proposal atop Empire State Building
items.standfirst: Pair also unfurled a large black banner displaying a message of peace on top of New York City landmarkTwo people were arrested following an apparent marriage proposal atop the Empire State Building’s spire on Wednesday, after they climbed to the very pinnacle of the New York City landmark – well above the level open to the public.The two people were identified as Angela Nikolau and Ivan Beerkus, two Russian “rooftoppers” who have conducted similar stunts in other cities including Tianjin, China, and Los Angeles. They were both wearing black clothing and appeared to be masked as they unfurled a large, black banner at the top of the skyscraper with the words: “When the power of love beats the love of power the ‌world knows peace.” Continue reading...
items.content: Pair also unfurled a large black banner displaying a message of peace on top of New York City landmarkTwo people were arrested following an apparent marriage proposal atop the Empire State Building’s spire on Wednesday, after they climbed to the very pinnacle of the New York City landmark – well above the level open to the public.The two people were identified as Angela Nikolau and Ivan Beerkus, two Russian “rooftoppers” who have conducted similar stunts in other cities including Tianjin, China, and Los Angeles. They were both wearing black clothing and appeared to be masked as they unfurled a large, black banner at the top of the skyscraper with the words: “When the power of love beats the love of power the ‌world knows peace.” Continue reading...

items.title: Two arrested after climbing Empire State Building antenna for marriage proposal
items.standfirst: Two people climbed the antenna of New York's Empire State Building on Wednesday, unfurled a banner promoting “the power of love” and appeared to stage a marriage proposal before being arrested. News footage showed the masked pair kissing atop the skyscraper before one knelt and the other appeared to display a ring.
items.content: Two people climbed the antenna of New York's Empire State Building on Wednesday, unfurled a banner promoting “the power of love” and appeared to stage a marriage proposal before being arrested. News footage showed the masked pair kissing atop the skyscraper before one knelt and the other appeared to display a ring.

items.content: Yorgen Fenech is charged with orchestrating the assassination of journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia.

items.title: Maltese businessman paid hitmen €150,000 to kill Daphne Caruana Galizia, jury hears
items.standfirst: Yorgen Fenech, heir to property empire, on trial for alleged involvement in murder of journalist, which he deniesOne of Malta’s wealthiest businessmen plotted to kill the investigative journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia, paying €150,000 (£130,000) for three hitmen to carry out the murder, a jury has heard.Yorgen Fenech, the 44-year-old heir to a property empire that includes the Hilton Malta hotel and casino, is on trial for the 2017 murder. Continue reading...
items.content: Yorgen Fenech, heir to property empire, on trial for alleged involvement in murder of journalist, which he deniesOne of Malta’s wealthiest businessmen plotted to kill the investigative journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia, paying €150,000 (£130,000) for three hitmen to carry out the murder, a jury has heard.Yorgen Fenech, the 44-year-old heir to a property empire that includes the Hilton Malta hotel and casino, is on trial for the 2017 murder. Continue reading...

items.title: Businessman goes on trial over murder of Maltese journalist
items.standfirst: Yorgen Fenech denies any involvement in the assassination of Daphne Caruana Galizia in 2017.
items.content: Yorgen Fenech denies any involvement in the assassination of Daphne Caruana Galizia in 2017.

items.title: Mexico scene shows crowds that killed three during World Cup celebration
items.standfirst: Two women and one man died of asphyxiation in downtown Mexico City as thousands of soccer fans celebrated their national team’s World Cup win over Ecuador.
items.content: Two women and one man died of asphyxiation in downtown Mexico City as thousands of soccer fans celebrated their national team’s World Cup win over Ecuador.

items.title: Four die in Mexico City World Cup celebrations
items.standfirst: More than one million people took to the streets of Mexico's capital on Tuesday to mark the country's win over Ecuador.
items.content: More than one million people took to the streets of Mexico's capital on Tuesday to mark the country's win over Ecuador.

items.title: Four people die in mass fan celebrations in Mexico City after World Cup victory
items.standfirst: Three of the victims died from suffocationMore than a million people gathered in Mexico CityFour people died, three ⁠from suffocation, as thousands of fans crowded Mexico City streets during World Cup celebrations, the capital’s health secretariat ⁠said in the early ⁠hours ​of Wednesday.The deaths occurred near the Angel of Independence landmark, where thousands of fans had gathered to ⁠celebrate Mexico’s 2-0 victory over Ecuador in the last 32. Continue reading...

items.title: Funerals held for 14 Pakistani children killed in tutoring center collapse
items.standfirst: Police are investigating whether negligence during construction work caused the collapse in the eastern city of Lahore on Tuesday.
items.content: Police are investigating whether negligence during construction work caused the collapse in the eastern city of Lahore on Tuesday.(Image credit: K.M. Chaudary)

items.title: Pakistan roof collapse kills 14 children at tutoring centre
items.standfirst: Local officials said preliminary reports showed the centre was unregistered and operating inside a privately owned residential building Fourteen children died after ⁠the roof of a tutoring centre collapsed in Pakistan’s eastern city of ⁠Lahore on Tuesday, ⁠rescue ​officials have said, as authorities opened the way for a possible negligence ⁠investigation.Punjab’s emergency service said rescuers found children and a 30-year-old female teacher ⁠under the rubble of the private after-school facility. The ​children killed were aged ‌five to ‌16 with most below nine. Continue reading...
items.content: Local officials said preliminary reports showed the centre was unregistered and operating inside a privately owned residential building Fourteen children died after ⁠the roof of a tutoring centre collapsed in Pakistan’s eastern city of ⁠Lahore on Tuesday, ⁠rescue ​officials have said, as authorities opened the way for a possible negligence ⁠investigation.Punjab’s emergency service said rescuers found children and a 30-year-old female teacher ⁠under the rubble of the private after-school facility. The ​children killed were aged ‌five to ‌16 with most below nine. Continue reading...

items.content: At least 14 children have been killed after the roof of an under-construction tutoring centre collapsed in Pakistan.

items.title: Russia unleashes huge bombardment of Kyiv
items.standfirst: Attack of 74 missiles and nearly 500 drones follows Zelenskyy’s warning that Moscow is preparing ‘massive strike’
items.content: Attack of 74 missiles and nearly 500 drones follows Zelenskyy’s warning that Moscow is preparing ‘massive strike’

items.title: Live: Russian missiles, drones kill residents, tear open buildings in 'massive attack' on Kyiv
items.standfirst: Russian missile and drone strikes tore open apartment buildings in Kyiv early Thursday, killing at least 17 people and wounding dozens, hours after President Volodymyr Zelensky warned that Moscow was preparing a "massive attack". Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: Russian missile and drone strikes tore open apartment buildings in Kyiv early Thursday, killing at least 17 people and wounding dozens, hours after President Volodymyr Zelensky warned that Moscow was preparing a "massive attack". Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Russia conducts deadly missile, drone strikes on Kyiv after Zelensky warns of ‘massive attack’
items.standfirst: Russian missile and drone strikes hit Kyiv early Thursday, sparking fires, killing 17 people and injuring dozens, Ukrainian officials said, after President Volodymyr Zelensky warned that Moscow was preparing a "massive attack".
items.content: Russian missile and drone strikes hit Kyiv early Thursday, sparking fires, killing 17 people and injuring dozens, Ukrainian officials said, after President Volodymyr Zelensky warned that Moscow was preparing a "massive attack".

clusters.neutral_headline: Google Loses EU Top Court Fight Over €4.1 Billion Android Fine
bullet_summary: 7 billion) European Union antitrust fine after the bloc’s top judges said regulators were right to punish the US giant for abusing Android’s market power

items.title: Google Loses EU Top Court Fight Over €4.1 Billion Android Fine
items.standfirst: Google lost its long-running fight against a €4.1 billion ($4.7 billion) European Union antitrust fine after the bloc’s top judges said regulators were right to punish the US giant for abusing Android’s market power.
items.content: Google lost its long-running fight against a €4.1 billion ($4.7 billion) European Union antitrust fine after the bloc’s top judges said regulators were right to punish the US giant for abusing Android’s market power.

items.title: EU top court rejects Google's appeal against record €4.1 billion antitrust fine
items.standfirst: The European Court of Justice on Thursday dismissed Google's appeal against a record fine imposed by EU antitrust regulators eight years ago for using its Android operating system to block rivals, in a ruling likely to boost Europe's crackdown on Big Tech.
items.content: The European Court of Justice on Thursday dismissed Google's appeal against a record fine imposed by EU antitrust regulators eight years ago for using its Android operating system to block rivals, in a ruling likely to boost Europe's crackdown on Big Tech.

clusters.title: OpenAI ‘in early talks to give 5% stake to US government’

Reported by Guardian World, Bloomberg Technology

items.title: OpenAI ‘in early talks to give 5% stake to US government’
items.standfirst: CEO Sam Altman argued move would share benefits of AI and it would involve other firms doing similar, report saysBusiness live – latest updatesOpenAI is reportedly in early stage talks to give a 5% stake in the ChatGPT developer to the US government as artificial intelligence companies attempt to smooth relations with Donald Trump’s administration.The OpenAI chief executive, Sam Altman, has argued that giving the US public a financial stake in the company is the best way to share the benefits of AI, according to the Financial Times, which cited two unnamed people familiar with the discussions. Continue reading...

clusters.title: FBI investigating legitimacy of Nancy Guthrie ransom notes
clusters.neutral_headline: FBI investigating legitimacy of Nancy Guthrie ransom notes
bullet_summary: The update comes a day after Reuters reported that kidnapping notes received in Guthrie’s case were fake
Reported by CBS News, Guardian World

items.title: FBI investigating legitimacy of Nancy Guthrie ransom notes
items.standfirst: The FBI said in a statement Wednesday that some ransom notes in Nancy Guthrie's disappearance have been "deemed to be extortion attempts without legitimacy," and other "demands may potentially be legitimate and are still being investigated as such."
items.content: The FBI said in a statement Wednesday that some ransom notes in Nancy Guthrie's disappearance have been "deemed to be extortion attempts without legitimacy," and other "demands may potentially be legitimate and are still being investigated as such."

items.title: Nancy Guthrie: FBI clarifies it is investigating some extortion demands as legitimate
items.standfirst: Statement comes a day after report that three messages received in case were determined to be fakeThe FBI is investigating extortion demands related to Nancy Guthrie’s disappearance that “may potentially be legitimate”, the agency said on Wednesday.The update comes a day after Reuters reported that kidnapping notes received in Guthrie’s case were fake. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Canada to ​join Eurovision song contest from 2027
clusters.neutral_headline: Canada to ​join Eurovision song contest from 2027
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: Canada to ​join Eurovision song contest from 2027
items.standfirst: Country is first to join since Australia in 2015 as event director says it ‘continues to welcome the world’Canada will ​join the Eurovision song contest in 2027, becoming the first new ⁠participant since Australia in 2015, organisers have announced.Participation is not limited to countries in geographic Europe and instead is open to all members of the European Broadcasting Union (EBU), which Canada joined last week. Australia is an associate member. Continue reading...
items.content: Country is first to join since Australia in 2015 as event director says it ‘continues to welcome the world’Canada will ​join the Eurovision song contest in 2027, becoming the first new ⁠participant since Australia in 2015, organisers have announced.Participation is not limited to countries in geographic Europe and instead is open to all members of the European Broadcasting Union (EBU), which Canada joined last week. Australia is an associate member. Continue reading...

items.title: Canada to make Eurovision Song Contest debut in 2027
items.standfirst: The country becomes the first new participant since Australia joined the contest in 2015.
